title: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Voice on Mac and Windows desktop, wired to Codex and Work agents
subtitle: GPT-Live-powered voice can now coordinate multiple background agents and read the frontmost app window on macOS.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: OpenAI released ChatGPT Voice for the macOS and Windows desktop applications on July 23. The mode uses GPT-Live — the full-duplex voice model OpenAI first shipped for iOS in early July — and lets users control the computer, direct multiple background agents in ChatGPT Work or Codex, and hold overlapping conversations, all with the microphone open. On macOS it can also read the frontmost application window, active files and codebase context alongside voice. The feature is rolling out globally on Plus, Pro, Business, Edu and Enterprise plans.

The interesting design choice is not that ChatGPT can now talk. It is that GPT-Live supports full duplex — the model can listen while it speaks, and multiple people in the same room can be in the same session simultaneously. OpenAI's launch video shows two Codex engineers in one room giving different instructions to the same agent in overlapping voice. That is a shift away from the single-user assistant model toward something that behaves like a shared collaborator. For teams already using Codex or ChatGPT Work agents to run background tasks, voice becomes the fastest way to steer several of them at once.

This is the third significant voice release from OpenAI in a month — mobile GPT-Live in early July, Realtime API updates on July 8, and now desktop. Competitors have been slower to ship comparable full-duplex quality. Anthropic's Claude voice mode remains a preview inside the mobile app. Google's Gemini Live is still catching up on latency for interruption handling. If GPT-Live holds its lead through Q4, voice-first agentic workflows on the desktop are OpenAI's to lose.

For learners: voice is going to change how much your typing speed matters at work. If a Codex engineer can direct three background agents in overlapping conversation, the bottleneck stops being how fast you can type prompts and starts being how well you can decompose a problem out loud. That is a different skill. Practice thinking through architectural decisions in speech, not just in written outlines — the ability to explain a design cleanly in one breath is about to become the substrate for how you drive coding agents. It rewards structured thinking more than fast fingers.

sources.title: Agentic coding goes hands-free as OpenAI brings GPT-Live's full duplex voice control to Codex and ChatGPT on the desktop — VentureBeat
OpenAI updating ChatGPT desktop app with GPT Voice for talking through work — 9to5Mac
OpenAI debuts ChatGPT Voice so you can have ongoing conversations, ask the AI to complete tasks while hands-free — Fortune
